Skip navigation links
  • Module
  • Package
  • Class
  • Tree
  • Index
  • Help
SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
AlreadyAssignedException
the alreadyAssignedException is a class that represents an Exception in case an object has already been assigned.
Back
The Back class represents the concept of the back side of a generic GameCard.
Bonus
The Bonus enumeration gives information on the way the points are assigned after placing a Front of a GoldCard.
Card
The Card abstract class represents the idea of a generic card of the game and serves as a blueprint for more specific cards.
CardCreator
The CardCreator class creates the list of Cards using a .json parser from the Gson library.
ChooseObjectiveMessage
Message sent by the client to the server to choose an objective card.
ChoosePrivateObjectiveController
Controller for the choose private objective scene.
ChooseStartCardController
Controller for the choose start card scene.
ChooseTokenController
This class is the controller for the chooseToken.fxml file It allows the player to choose a token from the available ones and sends it to the server.
ChooseTokenMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server to choose the token that the player wants to use during the game.
ClientConnectionInterface
the ClientConnectionInterface is an Interface implemented in the ClientRMI class and in the ClientTCP class to have the startClient method in both of them.
ClientHandler
This class is used to handle the connection with the client.
ClientInterface
Interface that defines the methods that the server can call on the client.
ClientMain
the ClientMain class handles the first connection of the client to the server.
ClientRMI
the ClientRMI class implements the RMIClientSkeleton and implements the method called by the player during the game.
ClientSideGameModel
This class is the client-side representation of the game model.
ClientSidePlayer
This class is a copy of the Player class, but it is used only for the client side.
ClientTCP
the ClientTCP class implements the ClientConnectionInterface and handles all the messages sent by the player.
Corner
The Corner class represents the concept of a generic corner of one of the sides of the card.
CreateGameController
This class is the controller for the createGame.fxml file.
CreationMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server when the client wants to create a new game.
Deck
The Deck abstract class represent the idea of a generic list of cards.
DeckCardDrawnMessage
This message is sent to the client when a card is drawn from the deck.
DiagonalDown
The DiagonalDown class represents the objective cards that require a descending diagonal (from left to right) of cards of the same colour in order to be fulfilled.
DiagonalUp
The DiagonalUp class represents the objective cards that require an ascending diagonal (from left to right) of cards of the same colour in order to be fulfilled.
Direction
The Direction enumeration is used to indicate where the Corner of the Card is located.
DrawAvailableMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server when the player wants to draw a card from the available cards.
DrawDeckMessage
Message used to draw a card from the deck.
ErrorMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when an error occurs.
Front
The Front class represents the concept of the front side of a generic GameCard.
GameCard
The GameCard abstract class represents a generic card that is placeable during the game and that has two sides.
GameController
The Class GameController's duty is to manage the phases of the game and to give directions to the model, maintaining the view updated.
GameModel
The GameModel class contains all the information and behaviour of an instance of the game.
GameOver
the GameOver class implements the State interface and manages the last state of the game.
GameStatus
the GameStatus is the enumeration of the possible status of the game.
GoldCard
The GoldCard class represents a generic gold card of the game.
GoldDeck
The GoldDeck class is a subclass of Deck that creates a list of GoldCards.
GUIController
This class is the superclass of all the controllers of the GUI.
GUIView
This class is the GUI view of the game.
GUIViewApplication
This class is the GUI view application of the game.
IncorrectUserActionException
the IncorrectUserActionException is a class that represents an Exception in case the controller can't execute a request of the player.
InitMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client to initialize the game.
JoinGameController
This class is the controller of the joinGame.fxml file It manages the join game scene
JoinMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server when the player wants to join a lobby.
Kingdom
The Kingdom class contains all the information and behaviour of the play area of a Player.
KingdomController
This class is the controller for the Kingdom.fxml file.
LobbiesListMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when the client asks for the list of lobbies.
LobbyController
This class is the controller of the lobby.fxml file It manages the lobby scene
LobbyRequestMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server when the player wants to see the list of lobbies.
LobbyState
the LobbyState class implements the State interface and is the first phase of the game, where the game is instanced.
LoginController
This class is the controller for the login.fxml file It handles the login screen, where the player can choose to create a new game or join an existing one
LShape
The LShape class represents the objective cards that require the cards to be placed to form an L in order to be fulfilled.
Message
This class is the abstract class that represents a message that can be sent through the network.
MessageId
Enumerates all the possible message types
MessageToClient
Abstract class that represents a message that is sent to the client
MessageToServer
Abstract class that represents a message that is sent to the server
MultipleMatchesHandler
the MultipleMatchesHandler class handles all the games at the same time.
NoCardsException
the NoCardsException is a class that represents an Exception in case there's no cards left in the deck.
NotifyMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when a player joins the lobby.
NowUnavailableMessage
This message is sent to the client when a token is no longer available.
ObjectiveCard
The ObjectiveCard abstract class represent a generic objective card and is used as a blueprint for specific objective cards.
ObjectiveDeck
The ObjectiveDeck class is a subclass of Deck that creates a list of ObjectiveCards.
Observable
This interface is used to implement the Observer pattern.
PingToClient
Message that is sent to the client to check if it is still connected
PingToServer
Message that is sent to the server to check if it is still connected
PlacementBoundObjective
The PlacementBoundObjective abstract class represent a generic placement bound objective card and is used as a blueprint for cards that require different placements.
PlaceMessage
This message is sent by the client to the server when the player wants to place a card.
Player
The Player class contains all the information and behaviour of a user of the application.
PlayerDisconnectedMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when a player disconnects.
PlayerJoinedMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when a player joins the lobby.
PlayerPoints
the PlayerPoints class contains the information about each player's points.
Position
The Position class contains the coordinates of the side of a card placed in the kingdom.
Resource
The Resource enumeration contains all the possible resources that can be obtained by a player during the game.
ResourceCard
The ResourceCard class represents a generic resource card of the game.
ResourceDeck
The ResourceDeck class is a subclass of Deck that creates a list of ResourceCards.
ResourcesBoundObjective
The ResourcesBoundObjective class represents the specific objective cards that require a certain amount of visible objects in the Kingdom to obtain the points indicated on the objective card.
ResultsMessage
the ResultsMessage class implements the MessageToClient Interface and handles the messages in the endgame that gives information about the resulting points of each player.
RMIClientSkeleton
the RMIClientSkeleton is the remote client seen by the server, that exposes the methods called by the server.
RMIServer
This class is the server that manages the RMI connections with the clients.
RMIServerStub
This interface is used by the server to communicate with the client.
RMIVirtualServer
This class is the RMI implementation of the VirtualServer interface.
RMIVirtualView
the RMIVirtualVIew implements the methods of the VirtualView Interface in case the player chooses an RMI connection.
Scoreboard
The Scoreboard class contains a table of players with their corresponding points and methods to interact with it.
ServerMain
This class is used to launch the server.
ServerTCP
This class is used to launch the server.
ShowResultsController
This class is the controller for the ShowResults.fxml file
Side
The Side abstract class represent the idea of a generic side of a card.
StandardCard
The StandardCard abstract class represents a generic gold card from the game, has two sides and has a main Resource, which can also be interpreted as his colour.
StartCard
The StartCard class represents a generic start card of the game.
StartDeck
The StartDeck class is a subclass of Deck that creates a list of StartCards.
State
the State interface represents each state of the game.
TCPVirtualServer
This class is the implementation of the VirtualServer interface for the TCP connection.
TCPVirtualView
the TCPVirtualVIew implements the methods of the VirtualView Interface in case the player chooses an RMI connection.
Token
The Token enumeration contains all the possible colours a player can choose.
TUIView
This class is the text user interface of the game.
TurnsMessage
This message is used to set the order of the players in the game.
UpdateAvailableView
This message is sent to the client to update the available cards in the view.
UpdateDeckView
This message is sent to the client to update the deck in the view.
UpdateKingdomMessage
This message is used to update the kingdom of a player.
UpdateLobbyMessage
This message is sent by the server to the client when a player joins the lobby.
View
This class is the abstract class that represents the view of the game.
ViewState
Enumerates the possible states of the view.
VirtualServer
This interface is used to define the methods that the VirtualServer must implement.
VirtualView
the VirtualView Interface exposes to the player the methods that the controller is able to call on the client's actual View, without knowing with which connection protocol said client is connected.
WaitDrawCard
the WaitDrawCard class implements the State interface and represents the action of drawing a card by the player.
WaitEndGameDraw
the WaitEndGameDraw class implements the State interface and represents the lasts drawings of each player.
WaitEndGamePlace
the WaitEndGamePlace class implements the state interface and represents the lasts placings of each player.
WaitObjective
the WaitObjective class implements the State interface and is the forth phase of the game.
WaitPlaceCard
the WaitPlaceCard class implements the State interface and represents the action of placing card by the player.
WaitStartCardSide
the WaitStartCardSide class implements the State interface and manages the second state of the game, in this state to the player will be given the starting card of which he will choose the side, and it will be initialized his kingdom.
WaitToken
the WaitToken class implements the State interface and manages the third phase of the game.
WrongGamePhaseException
the WrongGamePhaseException is a class that represents an Exception in case a player sends a message in the wrong phase of the game.
